essence: Uses scientific models to predict climate change impacts and inform environmental policy and adaptation strategies.

identity.title: Climate Modeller
identity.summary: Develops and runs climate models, analyses environmental data, and forecasts climate impacts to support research, policy, and climate‑risk planning.

salary.notes: Specialists in advanced modelling, climate‑risk analytics, or national research programmes can exceed £90k-£110k+.

qualifications: Training in climate science, environmental science, or atmospheric physics
Knowledge of climate models and simulation tools
Ability to analyse large datasets and interpret climate projections
Understanding of climate policy, adaptation, and mitigation

lifestyle.description: Climate modellers work for research institutes, universities, government agencies, and climate‑risk consultancies. They run simulations, analyse climate scenarios, and help organisations understand future risks. The role suits individuals who enjoy scientific modelling, data analysis, and long‑term environmental forecasting.

habits: Supports climate‑risk assessments and adaptation planning
Collaborates with scientists, policymakers, and sustainability teams
